data.content:
# Implementation Plan: Searchable Model Selection for CLI

## Problem
Both scripts/setup.sh and scripts/agent-wizard.sh present model lists as a numbered menu via _collect_discovered_models(). When OpenCode returns 175+ models, users must scroll through an enormous list with no way to search. The web UI already has search via cmdk; the CLI setup wizard does not.

## Approach: Bun-based interactive model selector

Create a TypeScript model selector script invoked from the bash scripts. This keeps the bash scripts lightweight while providing a real-time searchable TUI for large model lists.

Why not pure bash? Real-time filtering requires raw terminal mode and cursor control—fragile in bash, especially with Bash 3.2+ compatibility.

Why not fzf? Not a standard utility; adding an external dependency to the install flow is undesirable. The project already requires Bun.

## Architecture

bash script (_collect_discovered_models) -> if bun available & model count > 15 -> pipe models to scripts/select-models.ts -> reads stdin, presents interactive search TUI, outputs selections to stdout. Else: existing numbered list (fallback).

## Steps

### Step 1: Add CLI prompt library
Add @inquirer/search and @inquirer/checkbox (or @clack/prompts if it handles search+multiselect better) as devDependencies. Validate the chosen library works with Bun runtime. Fallback plan: build minimal TUI using Bun process.stdin raw mode + ANSI escape codes.

### Step 2: Create scripts/select-models.ts
Standalone Bun script (~80-120 lines):
- Reads model names from stdin (one per line)
- Presents searchable multi-select prompt
- Real-time case-insensitive substring matching
- Shows "X of Y models match" count
- Arrow keys to navigate, space to toggle, Enter to confirm
- Outputs selected model names to stdout (one per line)
- Exits cleanly on Ctrl-C (exit 130) or empty selection (exit 0)

### Step 3: Add bash helper function
Add _select_models_interactive() to both scripts:
- Resolves script path relative to BASH_SOURCE
- Checks bun on PATH and script file exists
- Pipes models to bun run select-models.ts
- Returns 1 on failure (signals fallback)

### Step 4: Modify _collect_discovered_models() in both scripts
- After building models array, check if count > 15
- If yes, attempt _select_models_interactive
- If interactive selector succeeds, use its output
- If it fails, fall back to existing numbered list
- Numbered list code stays intact as fallback

### Step 5: Testing
- Unit test (__tests__/select-models.test.ts): filter function correctness for "glm", "minimax", "qwen" (case-insensitive), empty search returns all, backspace restores full list
- Shell integration test: stdin/stdout contract, fallback when bun unavailable, exit codes
- Manual testing: opencode models | bun run scripts/select-models.ts with 175+ models

### Step 6 (optional): Deduplicate bash functions
Both scripts have near-identical _collect_discovered_models(). Consider extracting shared function into a helper sourced by both, if it doesn't break sourcing contract.

## Files Changed
- package.json — Add CLI prompt library as devDependency
- scripts/select-models.ts — NEW: interactive model selector
- scripts/setup.sh — Add _select_models_interactive(), modify _collect_discovered_models()
- scripts/agent-wizard.sh — Same changes
- __tests__/select-models.test.ts — NEW: unit tests

## Complexity
- Estimated effort: Medium (3-5 hours)
- Risk: Low-medium. Main risk is CLI prompt library compatibility with Bun. Mitigated by fallback to existing numbered list and option to build minimal TUI from scratch.

## Acceptance Criteria Mapping
- User can type search query -> interactive search TUI
- Real-time filtering -> library handles keystroke-by-keystroke
- Case-insensitive matching -> toLowerCase() substring match
- Backspace restores full list -> library handles natively
- Match count visible -> "X of Y models match" in prompt
- Responsive with 175+ models -> O(n) substring filter, instant
- Select via arrow keys -> library provides this
- No regression -> fallback to numbered list when bun unavailable or count <= 15
